zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Poges於大約1284年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾斯托克 波格斯時，她的父親Sir Robert Poges27歲，母親Amicia de Stokes23歲。她和Sir John de Mauduit至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1367年在英國享年84歲，埋葬在英國維爾爾格亞特 索莫福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Poges于大约1284年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾斯托克 波格斯时，她的父亲Sir Robert Poges27岁，母亲Amicia de Stokes23岁。她和Sir John de Mauduit至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1367年在英国享年84岁，埋葬在英国維爾爾格亞特 索莫福德。

nameMeanings.name: Poges
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Inge, Kurt.  Jewish Hillel, Mayer, Meir, Mendel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic from central Europe): metronymic from the Yiddish female personal name Porye, a derivative of the Biblical Zipporah, + the Yiddish or German possessive suffix -s.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Poges was born about 1284, in Stoke Poges, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Sir Robert Poges, was 27 and her mother, Amicia de Stokes, was 23. She had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Sir John de Mauduit. She died in 1367, in England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Great Somerford, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Poges est née environ 1284, à Stoke Poges, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Poges, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Amicia de Stokes, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Sir John de Mauduit. Elle est décédée en 1367, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Great Somerford, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Poges um 1284 in Stoke Poges, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Poges 27 und ihre Mutter Amicia de Stokes 23. Sie hatte mit Sir John de Mauduit mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1367 im Alter von 84 Jahren in England und wurde in Great Somerford, Wiltshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Pogesが約1284年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアストク ポジェズで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Pogesは27歳，母Amicia de Stokesは23歳であった。彼女はSir John de Mauduitとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1367年にイギリスで84歳で死去し，イギリスウィルトシュアイアグレアト ソメルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Poges(이)가 대략 1284년 영국 벅잉함실르 스토크 포그스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Poges(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Amicia de Stokes(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Sir John de Mauduit(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1367년 영국에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 윌츠히르 그리트 서머퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Poges родилась около 1284 г. в местности сток погесь, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Poges было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Amicia de Stokes, 23. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Sir John de Mauduit. Она умерла в 1367 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности грит сомирфорд, уилцхир, Англия.
